title: Re Purchasing End of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Re-Purchasing End of Evangelion             Quote Post by Zekkykins » Sun Sep 05, 2010 12:17 pm Well, awhile back I purchased a single-box bundle of EoE and De/Re, and I've had a couple of tiny, minor gripes with it. For starters, I'm a bit OCD, so to have them both in one single box.. Mildly irritates me. On top of that, the De/Re disc doesn't fit properly in it's holdy-thingy, and keeps on falling out. And, lastly, while it's not a real big issue, EoE suddenly ends before Asuka can finish her final line. It's literally "ki-MENU SCREEN, BITCHES." So, with those in mind, can anyone recommend me any specific copies of End of Evangelion that has fewer problems, and that preferably don't come bundles with De/Re?

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sun Sep 05, 2010 1:15 pm If you're referring to the Death & Rebirth/EoE box that Manga released a few years ago (which is what it sounds like), then I have the same product. It blows. However, I've never had the last scene of EoE ever cut off on me, so that sounds like a disc error. Or a bootleg. And there aren't any good releases of Death or EoE over here, yet. There's only the Manga Ent release, and it's atrocious. The hope is that Funimation will procure rights to the renewal versions of the films since they're the ones releasing Rebuild, but I haven't heard any news, yet. Both films were also sold separately by Manga, but since they're also out of print and identical to the boxed set (even the cases are the same), I don't recommend wasting money on them.

[]: Re: Re-Purchasing End of Evangelion             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Sep 05, 2010 1:23 pm Zekkykins wrote: Well, awhile back I purchased a single-box bundle of EoE and De/Re, and I've had a couple of tiny, minor gripes with it.

[]:             Quote Post by Zekkykins » Sun Sep 05, 2010 1:36 pm Ugh, yeah, looks like it's a bloody bootleg. ._. I'm really diggin' the look of the Manga release, though.. Very pretty box-art. But, yeah, what EoE release would y'all recommend I purchase? Or, do y'all really think I should stick it out and pray for a Funimation re-release?

[]: It's just the original Manga release, though.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Sun Sep 05, 2010 1:37 pm Yeah, I'd say you got a bootleg, unless there's a scratch on the disc or something. Anyway, I bought my copy of EoE from a seller on Amazon.com and I'm happy with it. You might want to check there.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Sep 05, 2010 1:57 pm Zekkykins wrote: But, yeah, what EoE release would y'all recommend I purchase?

[]:             Quote Post by Zekkykins » Sun Sep 05, 2010 2:05 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: The DVDs put out by Manga Entertainment were awful on a purely technical level.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sun Sep 05, 2010 2:13 pm Zekkykins wrote: What, exactly, was wrong with 'em?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Sep 05, 2010 2:20 pm Baz wrote: The video quality is even worse than you would expect from a bad VHS copy.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Sep 05, 2010 2:37 pm The video quality is horrible, we got "Reair" instead of "Rebirth" proper, sounds were replaced in the dub, mistranslations (Adam and Lilith are the same source lol) or lines NOT being translated (Shinji says to Asuka: "I want to help you somehow, and be with you forever." They rendered it as "I want to help you" and leave it at that! ) in both the dub and sub.... Yeah, not good. If you can't wait for a domestic release and would be willing to import, there is Renewal Death/EoE: http://www.cdjapan.co.jp/detailview.html?KEY=KIBA-1009 From what I understand, Gainax's original release of Death/Rebirth and EoE had the same poor quality as Mangle's release, but at least they rectified things over time with Renewal and such.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Sep 05, 2010 2:54 pm Zekkykins wrote: What, exactly, was wrong with 'em?

[]:             Quote Post by Zekkykins » Sun Sep 05, 2010 3:05 pm Well, that certainly explains the fact that the video is literally in a BOX, surrounded by black bars when I watch it on my HDTV.

[]:             Quote Post by SpearofLonginus » Sun Sep 05, 2010 4:38 pm Yeah, I didn't notice what crap quality this was until I watched it on my HDTV. This is from someone who doesn't know or care about qaulity.... End of Evangelion is what I love more than anything else in the Eva-verse, though and I'd prefer a better release... I WANT RENEWAL. I thought there was a sub using the renewal release around, but I'll leave it at that.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Sep 05, 2010 4:41 pm You, sir, have the same bootleg as me. I have all the same problems with my copy, and I've been meaning to get a new copy. However, whenever I have money I can't find it, and vice versa. Plus, it's not on my list of priorities right now.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sun Sep 05, 2010 10:27 pm The reason Manga released a non-anamorphic version of EoE is that as of its release date of Sept. 2002, there was no 16:9 home video version in existance. The Japanese KIBA-105 set from mid-1999 was also 4:3 letterbox, and it wouldn't be until the KIBA-91001 "monolith" boxset in mid-2003 (or the KIBA-1009 solo release a few months later) that an anamorphic Death or EoE would be on the home video market in Japan. Still, that doesn't excuse Manga from not putting out a better re-release while they still had the license later on... BTW, I recently bought the 2-movie R1 Manga boxset (basically the individual D+R and EoE releases in a slipcover box thingy picture above), making my current Manga R1 EoE copy redundant. I'd be willing to let it go for a relatively low price... /My apologies in advance if this sort of thing isn't allowed here...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Sep 05, 2010 10:37 pm svenge wrote: The reason Manga released a non-anamorphic version of EoE is that as of its release date of Sept. 2002, there was no 16:9 home video version in existance.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sun Sep 05, 2010 10:41 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: That is utter nonsense . Sure, they may have been given a non-anamorphic master, but they could have just made it anamorphic when they created their DVD. It's really fucking simple. It's a simple matter of project settings, and having the foresight to know that it needs to be anamorphic in order to display properly on widescreen format displays.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Sep 05, 2010 11:21 pm svenge wrote: I don't think that Manga thought all the way through on the non-anamorphic thing either, but then again: 1) Widescreen displays were quite rare in that era 2) Expecting coherent thought from Manga is like expecting a toddler to plan a bank robbery. Just not gonna happen.

[]:             Quote Post by Zekkykins » Mon Sep 06, 2010 12:19 am Well, let's face it, Manga has ALWAYS been terribly inconsistent. Hell, have you ever compared their first DVD collection of the original Guyver OVA series to their second one? The difference was honestly painful for me to watch, back when I got it. Wait, did Manga have any hand in the DVD release of Gurren Lagann? I noticed their logo attached to the episodes on Hulu.

[]:             Quote Post by RMZX » Mon Sep 06, 2010 1:14 am Zekkykins wrote: The difference was honestly painful for me to watch, back when I got it. Wait, did Manga have any hand in the DVD release of Gurren Lagann? I noticed their logo attached to the episodes on Hulu.

For starters, I'm a bit OCD, so to have them both in one single box.. Mildly irritates me. On top of that, the De/Re disc doesn't fit properly in it's holdy-thingy, and keeps on falling out. And, lastly, while it's not a real big issue, EoE suddenly ends before Asuka can finish her final line. It's literally "ki-MENU SCREEN, BITCHES." So, with those in mind, can anyone recommend me any specific copies of End of Evangelion that has fewer problems, and that preferably don't come bundles with De/Re?
